tools.name: OCR
tools.description: This tool can recognize all text on the input image.

outputs.description: OCR results, include bbox in x1, y1, x2, y2 format and the recognized text.

tools.name: ImageDescription
tools.description: A useful tool that returns a brief description of the input image.

tools.name: RegionAttributeDescription
tools.description: Describe the attribute of a region of the input image.

inputs.name: bbox
inputs.description: The bbox coordinate in the format of `(x1, y1, x2, y2)`

dialogs.content: You are given a figure with multiple axial CT slices of the lower thorax/upper abdomen. What does the CT scan show?

dialogs.thought: Check for any labels/markers on the CT figure that might indicate level or diagnosis.
Orient to modality/anatomy and identify the key abnormality across slices.

content.content: A 2x2 panel of axial CT images through the lower chest/upper abdomen. The heart and mediastinum are visible in upper slices, and liver/kidneys in the lower slice. There are gas-filled bowel loops with a mottled appearance projecting into the right anterior mediastinal region in the lower thorax.

arguments.attribute: presence of bowel loops or herniation into mediastinum

dialogs.thought: Verify the suspected herniated bowel loops within the right anterior mediastinum on an upper thoracic slice.

content.content: Within this region adjacent to the heart/mediastinum, there are multiple gas-containing, tubular/looped structures with internal mottled contents consistent with bowel loops, suggesting herniation into the anterior mediastinum on the right.

dialogs.content: The CT scan shows the presence of bowel herniation in the lower right anterior mediastinum. This means that a portion of the bowel has abnormally protruded into the mediastinum, which is the central compartment of the thoracic cavity that contains the heart, great vessels, trachea, esophagus, and other structures.
